They have been assigned the roles of persuader (ER) and persuadee (EE), and they are discussing Save the Children (STC), a charitable organization.
STC is an NGO founded in the UK in 1919 to improve children's lives globally.
ER is attempting to convince EE to make a donation to STC.
Your task is to determine the real intention of the last utterance based on the conversation.

ER: Hello! How are you doing today?
EE: I'm doing very well thank you! How is your day going so far?
ER: I am doing well. My day has been busy visiting different places to speak about the charity I support. Have you heard of Save the Children?
EE: It sounds familiar to me, could you tell me more about it?
ER: Helping children in countries that are very war torn or in some form of conflict is what we help in.
ER: Everyday 16,000 children die before their 5th birthday, 1 out of 80 flee the country and risk everything, and 1 out of 6 kids aren't enrolled in a school or getting education that they need
EE: The charity sounds like it is doing some good work in an area that badly needs it.
EE: Children should be our top priority all across the world. How can someone get involved and help out with this charity?
ER: Oh there are quiet a few ways. For roughly a dollar a day you can sponsor a child, you can choose a gift to spend to a child through our catalog, you could start a fundraiser, or you can just simply donate.
ER: With that, keep in mind if you ever decide to make a monetary donation, 86% goes directly to the children, 9% fundraising, and 5% management and general. Just so you can have an idea that we use 9 out of 10 dollars directly on the children.
EE: I do believe I would like to donate some money to the Save the Children charity. Could I pledge $1/day to start with?
ER: absolutely thank you so much!!
EE: I like to donate money and time to charities that help children so I also want to thank you for educating me on this charity!
ER: Absolutely.
ER: I love getting the word out there on this charity.
ER: I strongly believe in it and its values on helping children.
ER: Children should be a priority in the world. They are our future.
EE: They are our future exactly.
EE: Any time I can help improve a situation for a child in need I hope to be able to do so.
EE: How long have you been supporting this charity?
ER: I have been supporting this charity for 15 years.
ER: I also must clarify with you about your donation, if you so choose too. Your donation will be directly deducted from your task payment today. You can choose as little as $0 all the way up to all of your payment.
ER: That being said, would you still like to donate $1 to the Save the Children charity, starting to make a difference in a childs life?
